content.text:
```mdl
@ NON-NEGOTIABLE CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT – TRANSPORTATION CHALLENGE 2017
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_9_nda_11

open std.collections

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Party – generic participant in the agreement
type Party = {
    name: string,
    address: string,
    contact_email: string
}

@ Confidential information that is shared between the parties
type ConfidentialInfo = {
    data_type: string,          # e.g. "student routing data"
    description: string
}

@ Security measures that must be present in the Vendor's database
type SecurityMeasure = {
    name: string                 # e.g. "encryption", "firewall", "anti-virus"
}

@ Database used by the Vendor to store Confidential Information
type Database = {
    security_measures: List<SecurityMeasure>
}

@ Vendor – the receiving party (the “Receiving Party”)
type Vendor = {
    name: string,
    # Boolean flags that capture the Vendor's compliance state
    has_disclosed_confidential: bool,
    has_reverse_engineered: bool,
    has_returned_confidential: bool,
    has_notified_breach: bool,
    has_signed_nda: bool
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ The Boston Public Schools (the “Disclosing Party”)
entity bps: Party

@ The Vendor (the “Receiving Party”)
entity vendor: Vendor

@ The set of Confidential Information covered by the Agreement
entity confidential_info: ConfidentialInfo

@ The Vendor's secure database
entity database: Database

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper Functions
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Checks whether a given security measure is present in the database
func has_security_measure(db: Database, measure_name: string) -> bool:
    case db.security_measures:
        | Cons(m, rest) =>
            if m.name = measure_name then true
            else has_security_measure({ security_measures = rest }, measure_name)
        | Empty(_) => false

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules – Obligations (O), Prohibitions (F), Permissions (P)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 2.1 – Vendor must keep Confidential Information secret
rule F vendor_must_not_disclose_confidential:
    vendor.has_disclosed_confidential always

@ Clause 2.1 – Vendor shall not reverse‑engineer any objects embodying Confidential Information
rule F vendor_must_not_reverse_engineer:
    vendor.has_reverse_engineered always

@ Clause 2.6 – Vendor must return or destroy Confidential Information when requested
rule O vendor_must_return_or_destroy_confidential:
    vendor.has_returned_confidential always

@ Clause 2.4 – Vendor must give BPS immediate notice of any unauthorized use or breach
rule O vendor_must_notify_breach_immediately:
    vendor.has_notified_breach always

@ Clause 2.1 – Vendor must have signed the NDA before accessing data
rule O vendor_must_have_signed_nda:
    vendor.has_signed_nda always

@ Clause 2.1 – Database must include required security safeguards
rule O database_must_include_required_security_measures:
    has_security_measure(database, "encryption") and
    has_security_measure(database, "firewall") and
    has_security_measure(database, "anti-virus") and
    has_security_measure(database, "access-authentication") always

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# End of module
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
```
